original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with cataracts, including people considering referral or surgery and people receiving postoperative review.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, optometric, ophthalmology, surgical, and postoperative cataract care.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for selected cataract identity, vision and quality-of-life impact, laterality, preference-sensitive surgery discussion, postoperative information, visual-function review, and clinician-arranged follow-up fields. It does not support autonomous diagnosis, surgery or referral, medicine, dose, fixed review timing, or UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.2 — cataract information, visual function, quality of life, and referral discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3 — postoperative information and assessment
exact_sections.locator: recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3; official page lines 101–136 and 473–489
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.

title: Cataracts in adults: management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Adults aged 18 years and over with cataracts, including people considering referral or surgery and people receiving postoperative review.
intended_setting: Primary, optometric, ophthalmology, surgical, and postoperative cataract care.
